source.title: Multiple Scales of Representation along the Hippocampal Anteroposterior Axis in Humans

confidence_rationale: Hippocampus itself shows anteroposterior gradient of temporal scales: anterior HC has slower signal change (coarse-grained), posterior HC has faster signal change (fine-grained). Confirms multi-scale segmentation is not just cortical (Baldassano) but extends into the hippocampus where MECH-269 anchors live. Validates Q5: place-cell-field-scale (posterior HC fast scale) and event-segment scale (anterior HC slow scale) coexist as parametric variants of one anatomically-graded substrate, NOT as separate substrates.
failure_signatures: Anterior HC slower autocorrelation, posterior HC faster. A substrate where MECH-269 anchor-set is single-scale would lose the natural anatomical multi-scale support.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Anterior hippocampus shows slower BOLD signal change and higher autocorrelation than posterior hippocampus during navigation and rest, mirroring the rodent dorsal/ventral gradient of place-field size. The gradient supports an anterior-to-posterior gradient of coarse-to-fine spatiotemporal representations.
mapping.ree_translation: Q5 verdict directly addressed: place-cell-field scale (posterior HC, fine) and event-segment / schema scale (anterior HC, coarse) are parametric variants of ONE anatomically-graded substrate, NOT separate substrates. The MECH-288 event_segmenter should use the same boundary-detection algorithm with different time-constant parameters per scale, rather than implementing separate detectors for place-cell-field-scale and event-segment-scale boundaries.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The gradient is observed in human BOLD; the substrate-level analogue (different time constants in different anchor-set keys) is an architectural inference. The paper does not directly test multi-scale event-segmentation but the timescale gradient is a strong indirect support.
mapping.source_context: fMRI of healthy human adults during navigation and rest; voxel-time-course autocorrelation analysis.

confidence_components.notes: Current Biology, well-controlled. Mapping high for the architectural Q5 verdict (parametric refinement vs separate substrate).
